bids.reasoning: I am targeting my maximum-utility opening bid since we are at t=0.00 and there is no time pressure to concede yet. The opponent’s offer gives me very low utility, and their message is a threat rather than evidence of real concession limits, so I will anchor on my strongest values across all issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still the opening phase at t=0.02, and the opponent has only repeated an extreme all-valueF offer that is very poor for me. Their messages are threats/urgency, but the offer history shows no real concession yet, so I will make only a small concession from my prior all-valueA position while avoiding an exact repeat.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.94 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain extremely low-value for me. Their message claims movement on issueD, but numerically that move is worse for my utility, so I am treating the threats as cheap talk and making only a modest concession on issueC while holding firm on my most important issueA.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06, and the opponent has shown almost no meaningful concession—their latest move on issueD actually lowers my utility compared with valueF. I will make only a small concession from my prior bid by moving issueC from valueD to valueC, while holding firm on the more important issueA and strong values on issueB/issueD; their threats are cheap talk this early.

I am still targeting a high utility because it is very early (t=0.08) and the opponent’s offers remain low for me despite minor movement on issueC/issueD. Their threats about walking away are cheap talk, but their latest offer suggests issueC may be a focal concession area, so I keep my high-weight issueA and strong positions on issueB/issueD while offering a moderate issueC concession without repeating my immediately prior bid.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making a modest concession on issueC, where I can give valueB with limited loss. The opponent’s messages are mostly threats, and their actual offers remain very low utility for me, with only small/uneven movement, so I will not concede on the higher-priority issueA or on issueB/issueD yet.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and their threats appear to be cheap talk, though they have signaled some movement on issueC and issueD; I will concede mainly on issueC, which is the least costly concession among my high-value options, while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid of about 0.856 out of my 0.90 maximum, since we are still early at t=0.14 and there is no need to rush toward the opponent’s low-value anchors. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me and their threats are cheap talk; they have shown some movement on issueA and issueC, so I make a modest concession on issueC while holding firm on my most important positions.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 rather than accepting the opponent’s very low offers to me. The opponent has made only small concessions, mainly on issueC and occasionally issueA/issueD, while issueB remains consistently valueF, so I will hold firm on high-value issues for me and reciprocate on issueC. Their threats to walk are cheap talk and not supported by the early time fraction.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid while making a clearer concession than my prior counters, since we are still early at t=0.18 and the opponent’s threats are not credible enough to force a major drop. Their offers remain low for me and mostly hold issueB at valueF, but they have varied issueA/issueC/issueD; I will hold firm on high-importance issueA and issueB while conceding issueD to valueF and keeping issueC at a moderate valueD.

At t=0.20 I am still targeting a fairly high utility bid, around 0.76, because there is plenty of time left and the opponent’s offers remain much lower for me despite small concessions. Their bids consistently hold issueB at valueF, so I am making a meaningful concession there while protecting my most important issueA and restoring value on issueC and issueD; I treat their repeated walk-away threats as cheap talk rather than binding.

I am targeting a still-strong utility while making a visible concession on issues the opponent has repeatedly emphasized through offers, especially issueB and issueC. At t=0.22 it is still early enough to hold firm on my highest-value issueA and issueD, and the opponent’s threats are cheap talk despite some small concessions in their bids.

I am targeting a still-positive but clearly more conciliatory bid around 0.63 utility, since we are only at t=0.24 but the gap remains large. The opponent’s messages are mostly threats and urgency, so I rely on the bid history: they repeatedly push issueB toward valueF and issueC toward valueC/F, while occasionally moving issueA to valueD/valueE; I therefore concede on issueB and issueC and partially on issueA while holding issueD at my best value.

I am targeting a moderate concession with utility about 0.535, still well above reservation while responding to the opponent’s repeated signals. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and their threats look like cheap talk at t=0.26, but they have repeatedly shown interest in issueA=valueD/F, issueB=valueF/E, and issueC=valueC, so I concede on those while holding issueD=valueA as an important remaining point.

I am targeting roughly 0.50 utility: a meaningful concession from my earlier offers, but still well above the opponent’s latest low-utility bid since we are only at t=0.28. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk; their actual offers show they strongly favor valueF/valueE, with some movement on issueA and issueC, so I am conceding issueB, issueC, and issueD toward their pattern while preserving some value on my highest-weight issueA.
